behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily deterministic backoff math/state progression. A pure in-process test can drive consecutive failures and assert delay sequence and cap enforcement without I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Test the retry delay calculator/state machine directly: simulate N consecutive retryable failures, assert delays are [base, 2x, 4x, ...] and never exceed maxBackoffMs.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is at the boundary between HTTP response interpretation and retry policy (e.g., status/header parsing mismatch). A unit mock of one function may miss contract mismatches between modules.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry middleware with the real response-classification code and a stub transport returning realistic 429 responses (including/omitting Retry-After). Assert it classifies as retryable and computes expected wait from headers/fallback backoff.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: This validates orchestration across retry scheduler + request executor + response propagation. Mocking only one piece can hide dropped/mutated request or result-mapping bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake timers plus a stub transport: first call returns 429, second call returns success payload. Assert transport receives the same request twice (same method/url/body/headers), and `call()` resolves to the final payload.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Error typing is a cross-module outcome of retry exhaustion + error translation. Unit tests on the error class alone would miss wrong throw site or generic wrapping.
behaviors.test_description: Configure maxRetries to a small value; stub transport always returns rate-limit responses. Execute `call()` and assert rejection is instance of `QuotaExhaustedError` with expected metadata, and not a generic Error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is control-flow across multiple modules and async scheduling. You need the assembled retry loop/quota tracker to catch non-terminating logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real retry chain with always-rate-limited stub responses and fake timers advanced through all backoff windows. Assert total attempts == 1 + maxRetries, promise settles within bounded virtual time, and final error is surfaced once.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: This is shared-state/concurrency behavior across caller-facing API and quota manager. In-process but multi-module; unit scope is too narrow to expose real contention bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate one client, fire many concurrent `call()` invocations, and use a deterministic stub transport/quota limiter to record token consumption. Assert aggregate consumption respects one shared bucket across all callers (not per-call isolated buckets).

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: FIFO ordering emerges from queueing + scheduler + async wake-up behavior together. Mocking queue internals alone can miss dispatch-order regressions in real wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Submit multiple calls rapidly while quota is exhausted, then release quota in controlled steps. Capture dispatch timestamps/order at transport boundary and assert strict submission-order dispatch (FIFO).
